manifest.name: FortiGate Config Push
manifest.description:
Pushes FortiOS CLI configuration to a FortiGate device via REST API.

Translates CLI config blocks into API calls for automated deployment.
Supports all major config sections:
- System interfaces and global settings
- IPsec Phase1/Phase2 tunnels
- SD-WAN zones, members, health-checks, neighbors
- BGP neighbors, neighbor-groups, neighbor-ranges
- Firewall addresses and policies
- Static routes

This tool closes the automation gap - AI can now fully deploy
new SD-WAN sites without human intervention.

Use with:
1. fortigate-sdwan-blueprint-planner (generate config)
2. fortigate-config-push (this tool - push config)
3. fortigate-sdwan-status (verify tunnels)
4. fortigate-sdwan-manifest-tracker (track device)

config_path.description: Path to FortiOS CLI config file

config_text.description: FortiOS CLI config as string (alternative to config_path)

dry_run.description: Parse config only, don't push to device

security.notes: Version 1.0.2 - Re-submit after Trust Anchor sync issue

skills_content:
# FortiGate Config Push

## Purpose
Push FortiOS CLI configuration to a FortiGate device via REST API. Enables 100% automated SD-WAN deployment without human intervention.

## When to Use
- Deploying a new SD-WAN spoke/hub from blueprint
- Applying bulk configuration changes
- Automating FortiGate provisioning
- Any scenario where you have CLI config to push

## The Automation Gap This Closes

```
BEFORE (Human in loop):
  Blueprint â†’ CLI Config â†’ [USER PASTES] â†’ FortiGate

AFTER (100% AI):
  Blueprint â†’ CLI Config â†’ [THIS TOOL] â†’ FortiGate
```

## Parameters

### Required
| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| target_ip | string | FortiGate management IP |

### Config Source (one required)
| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| config_path | string | Path to CLI config file |
| config_text | string | CLI config as string |

### Optional
| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| dry_run | boolean | false | Parse only, don't push |

## Usage Examples

### Push Config from File
```json
{
  "target_ip": "192.168.1.99",
  "config_path": "C:\\ProgramData\\Ulysses\\config\\blueprints\\Branch3_config.txt"
}
```

### Push Config from String
```json
{
  "target_ip": "192.168.1.99",
  "config_text": "config system global\n  set hostname \"Branch3\"\nend"
}
```

### Dry Run (Parse Only)
```json
{
  "target_ip": "192.168.1.99",
  "config_path": "C:\\ProgramData\\Ulysses\\config\\blueprints\\Branch3_config.txt",
  "dry_run": true
}
```

## Full SD-WAN Deployment Workflow

### Step 1: Generate Blueprint
```json
// fortigate-sdwan-blueprint-planner
{
  "action": "generate-template"
}
// User fills in WAN/LAN details in CSV
```

### Step 2: Generate Config
```json
// fortigate-sdwan-blueprint-planner
{
  "action": "plan-site",
  "csv_path": "C:\\...\\Branch3_template.csv"
}
// Returns: Branch3_config.txt
```

### Step 3: Push Config (THIS TOOL)
```json
// fortigate-config-push
{
  "target_ip": "192.168.1.99",
  "config_path": "C:\\...\\Branch3_config.txt"
}
```

### Step 4: Verify Tunnels
```json
// fortigate-sdwan-status
{
  "target_ip": "192.168.1.99"
}
// Verify: HUB1-VPN1 UP, HUB1-VPN2 UP
```

### Step 5: Absorb to Manifest
```json
// fortigate-sdwan-manifest-tracker
{
  "action": "absorb",
  "target_ip": "192.168.1.99"
}
```

## Supported Config Sections

| CLI Section | API Endpoint | Status |
|-------------|--------------|--------|
| system global | /api/v2/cmdb/system/global | âœ“ |
| system interface | /api/v2/cmdb/system/interface | âœ“ |
| vpn ipsec phase1-interface | /api/v2/cmdb/vpn.ipsec/phase1-interface | âœ“ |
| vpn ipsec phase2-interface | /api/v2/cmdb/vpn.ipsec/phase2-interface | âœ“ |
| router static | /api/v2/cmdb/router/static | âœ“ |
| router bgp | /api/v2/cmdb/router/bgp | âœ“ |
| firewall address | /api/v2/cmdb/firewall/address | âœ“ |
| firewall policy | /api/v2/cmdb/firewall/policy | âœ“ |
| system sdwan | /api/v2/cmdb/system/sdwan | âœ“ |

## Output Example

### Success
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "target": "192.168.1.99",
  "blocks_total": 15,
  "blocks_success": 15,
  "blocks_failed": 0,
  "pushed_at": "2026-01-17T18:30:00.000000",
  "results": [
    {"success": true, "section": "system global", "entry": null},
    {"success": true, "section": "system interface", "entry": "port1"},
    {"success": true, "section": "vpn ipsec phase1-interface", "entry": "HUB1-VPN1"}
  ]
}
```

### Partial Failure
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "target": "192.168.1.99",
  "blocks_total": 15,
  "blocks_success": 14,
  "blocks_failed": 1,
  "results": [
    {"success": false, "section": "firewall policy", "entry": "1",
     "result": {"http_status": 500, "error": "Policy conflict"}}
  ]
}
```

### Dry Run
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "mode": "dry_run",
  "target": "192.168.1.99",
  "blocks_parsed": 15,
  "blocks": [
    {"section": "system global", "entry": null},
    {"section": "system interface", "entry": "port1"},
    {"section": "system interface", "entry": "port3"}
  ]
}
```

## Error Handling

| Error | Cause | Solution |
|-------|-------|----------|
| No API token | Device not in credentials | Add to fortigate_credentials.yaml |
| HTTP 401 | Invalid token | Check API token permissions |
| HTTP 404 on PUT | Entry doesn't exist | Tool auto-retries with POST |
| HTTP 500 | Config conflict | Check FortiGate logs |

## Prerequisites

1. **API Token**: Device must have API token in credentials file
2. **Network Access**: Tool must reach FortiGate on HTTPS/443
3. **API Access**: FortiGate must have API access enabled

## Related Tools
- `fortigate-sdwan-blueprint-planner` - Generate config
- `fortigate-sdwan-status` - Verify tunnels
- `fortigate-sdwan-manifest-tracker` - Track device
- `fortigate-health-check` - Basic health check

## Security Notes
- Uses existing credential store (no secrets in parameters)
- All API calls over HTTPS
- Supports SSL verification skip for lab environments
